title: phaseolus vulgaris var. nankinensis
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. nankinensis, also known as the Nankin kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean plant (Phaseolus vulgaris), specifically bred for its compact growth and edible seeds. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi, making it a potential natural remedy for infections. The seeds of the Nankin kidney bean have been reported to provide health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as potential anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds of Phaseolus vulgaris var. nankinensis are often dried and powdered, then used in teas, capsules, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
